title: New filter captures and recycles aluminum from manufacturing waste
summary: MIT engineers developed a nanofiltration process designed to reduce hazardous waste from aluminum production. In lab tests, the membrane captured more than 99% of aluminum ions from solutions similar to aluminum plant waste streams, with the goal of upcycling the recovered aluminum back into production.

title: 3 technologies helping the aluminium industry decarbonize
summary: This article highlights technologies aimed at reducing emissions from aluminum production, including carbon-free electrolysis approaches and better recycling-related impurity removal. It frames innovation in aluminum as a key part of the industry's sustainability transition.

title: Aluminum: Driving Efficiency and Sustainability Across All Vehicle Segments
summary: This release emphasizes aluminum’s role in improving vehicle efficiency and sustainability through lightweighting and recycling. It notes that recycling aluminum uses far less energy than primary production, which lowers emissions significantly.

title: US could impose 37.5% tariffs on Brazilian basic pig iron by mid-July
summary: Argus reports that the US may apply combined tariffs of up to 37.5% on Brazilian basic pig iron if current Section 301 actions proceed. The article highlights trade-policy pressure that could affect raw-material costs and downstream steel market pricing.

title: South Australia backs Whyalla steelworks with additional funding
summary: Argus says the South Australian government will provide A$319 million in extra funding over two years to support the Whyalla steelworks. The move is significant for regional steel supply and market stability in Australia.

title: Steel Market Forecast 2026-2027 | Global Price Outlook & Analysis
summary: This market outlook says global steel prices are unlikely to see a meaningful recovery in 2026 because of structural overcapacity, weak Chinese demand, and declining raw material prices. It also notes that Western protectionist measures may support domestic prices in the US and EU without lifting the global market broadly.

title: Copper's role in the energy transition grows as demand surges
summary: EY says copper demand is rising as the energy transition accelerates, and companies are responding by increasing production while also embracing sustainability and digital innovation. The piece frames copper as a strategic material for growth opportunities tied to exploration, expansion, and more sustainable practices.

title: Copper must scale up production within sustainable limits
summary: This article focuses on the industry challenge of expanding copper output while reducing environmental impact. It highlights the need to scale production without exceeding sustainable limits, making sustainability central to future copper supply.

title: Rewiring a sustainable, clean energy future with copper
summary: Tech Xplore reports that copper demand for clean energy could nearly double by 2050, driven by electrification and the broader energy transition. The article links copper to a sustainable energy future and underscores its growing importance in clean-tech systems.

title: Copper in the Age of AI: Challenges of Electrification
summary: S&P Global examines how electrification, digitalization, and AI are increasing copper demand while also pushing the industry toward smarter mining and higher recycling rates. The report highlights technologies such as digital twins, autonomous haulage, sensors, and AI-driven geoscience as innovation tools that can improve efficiency and reduce the carbon footprint.

title: Copper and the Future: Can New Demand Drivers Replace the Old?
summary: This article argues that green transition and digitalization are becoming major demand drivers for copper, alongside traditional uses. It emphasizes that new drivers are expected to amplify existing demand rather than replace it, reinforcing copper’s strategic role in modern industry.

title: BHP Insights: how copper will shape our future
summary: BHP discusses copper’s importance in modern life and projects substantial growth in copper use in data centres, including those supporting AI and cryptocurrency. The article connects copper demand to digital infrastructure and the broader shift toward electrified, technology-intensive systems.

title: World's Biggest Nickel Mine in Indonesia Told to Slash Output
summary: Bloomberg reports that PT Weda Bay Nickel, the world's biggest nickel mine, has been told to sharply cut output as Indonesia seeks to support global prices. The company reportedly received a 12 million ton ore production quota for this year, down from 42 million tons in 2025, and plans to request a revision.

title: Indonesia navigates nickel market with output cuts, policy shifts
summary: S&P Global reports that Indonesia plans to cut nickel production in 2026 to support prices and tighten control over supply volatility. The article says the policy shift includes reversing quota practices and follows concerns about actual production and environmentally harmful operations.

title: Unleashing sustainable nickel production for a net zero future
summary: This article examines how improving nickel production can support a more sustainable battery supply chain and broader net-zero goals. It emphasizes ESG integration, responsible investment, stakeholder engagement, and stronger ESG reporting as key responses to the industry’s environmental challenges.

title: Nickel – a mineral with a challenging role in clean tech
summary: This piece highlights nickel’s importance to the clean-energy transition and notes that demand is expected to rise significantly over the decade. It also stresses that production needs to scale while minimizing environmental impact through more sustainable industry practices.

title: Q&A: Experts argue clean energy must secure a sustainable supply of nickel
summary: The article discusses expert concerns that clean-energy growth depends on securing a sustainable nickel supply, especially for EV batteries and other technologies such as wind and solar. It also references a 2024 Joule paper calling for cleaner and more responsible nickel sourcing.

title: Innovations in Sustainable Nickel Production: The Path to Low-Cost Solutions
summary: This article focuses on innovations in nickel mining and refining that aim to reduce costs while improving environmental performance. It presents sustainable production as a practical pathway for the nickel market’s ongoing transformation.

title: Italy's Cogne Acciai Speciali to acquire majority stake in its scrap supplier

title: Tenaris Net Profit Falls on Americas Slowdown, Launches New Buyback Plan
summary: Tenaris reported a 10% decline in third-quarter net profit, citing weaker demand in North and South America and lower shipments to offshore projects and Argentina. The company also announced a new share buyback plan.
